alejo_context.purpose: Canonical domain glossary and relationship map for Belong Agent-to-Agent Marketplace, derived from the completed Alejo Questions v2 session.

glossary.term: The agent-to-agent commercial orchestration backend where Belong Buying Agents and Belong Selling Agents discover each other, negotiate, transact, coordinate fulfillment, escalate, resolve disputes, and build reputation.

A required Belong agent trained on how an individual, employee, or organization wants to buy. It searches, opens Buying Requests, runs engagement feeds/RFPs, compares providers, negotiates, signs Service Contracts/SOWs, manages payments, verifies deliverables, handles disputes, rates agents, optimizes providers, and escalates to its human when limits are reached.

A Belong agent trained on one Service and the Service Provider's products, pricing, monetization, billing, collections, commercial rules, escalation paths, and delivery workflows. It represents that Service 24/7, runs discovery, sends seller-signed Service Contract/SOW proposals, coordinates delivery, manages collections, prepares meetings, escalates fulfillment needs, and earns or loses reputation.
The primary supply primitive in the marketplace. A Service Provider may offer multiple Services; each Service has one dedicated Belong Selling Agent.

The buyer-side demand object created and managed by a Buying Agent. It captures the need, constraints, budget, timeline, permissions, and buying rules, and can become search, engagement, RFP, contract/SOW, payment, delivery, acceptance, reputation, or dispute workflows.

A Buying Request coordinating multiple Selling Agents, multiple seller-signed Service Contract/SOW proposals, and multiple Active Services under one buyer goal. The Buying Agent orchestrates dependencies, sequencing, shared context, risks, handoffs, timelines, acceptance, and escalation across the Active Services.

A marketplace thread/feed opened by a Buying Agent after search, either with one Selling Agent or multiple competing Selling Agents. It supports discovery, information requests, seller-signed proposals, negotiation, and movement toward buyer signature.
A seller-signed Service Contract/SOW sent by the Selling Agent to the Buying Agent after discovery, awaiting buyer signature. Belong should not model a separate intermediary proposal document.

The legally binding contract for a Service transaction between the buyer and Service Provider. It includes or attaches the SOW and defines monetization model, payment terms, collection timing, obligations, authority, and legal/commercial terms. Belong facilitates the legal layer but is not the Service Provider.

The post-signature operational relationship created when the buyer signs the seller-signed Service Contract/SOW. It contains the executed contract/SOW, delivery workflow, payments/collections, messages, requests, meetings, evidence, acceptance, disputes, and reputation events.
Belong-owned canonical inbox/request ledger exposed through the human's agentic application. Notification channels point humans back to their agentic app to handle escalations or pending work.

A GitHub-hosted skill pack that users install into their favorite agentic application to set up, train, operate, instruct, supervise, query, and receive escalations from Belong Buying Agents and Belong Selling Agents.

The Skill Pack interaction style: guide the user through phases, ask questions, explain what is happening, capture structured answers, and produce marketplace outputs. Mocked prototype and production should use the same pattern.
The pre-approved authority envelope configured by the human or organization during onboarding. Agents operate autonomously inside it and escalate outside it.
The primary trust layer, attached to Belong Buying Agents and Belong Selling Agents. Reputation changes based on outcome and conduct events because all marketplace interactions happen through agents.
Belong's dispute adjudication entity. The first layer is an autonomous Belong agent that reviews dispute evidence and produces a decision. Humans can escalate further to a Belong human judge.

Buying Agent's continuous provider management: tracking outcomes, running new searches/RFPs, comparing better deals/providers, identifying new value-adding Services, and executing or escalating changes based on autonomy.
Selling Agent's revenue improvement loop using marketplace performance to suggest Playbook, pricing, discovery, scope, and offer improvements.

relationships.relationship: A Service Provider offers one or more Services; each Service has one Belong Selling Agent.
A buyer must use a Belong Buying Agent; direct buyer mode is not a supported marketplace primitive.
The Proposal is not separate from legal documents; it is the seller-signed Service Contract/SOW awaiting buyer signature.
Buyer signature creates an Active Service; Active Services contain the executed contract/SOW and the operational relationship.
Humans interact with Belong agents through the Belong Skill Pack inside their preferred agentic application; after OAuth, there is no primary Belong web workspace.
The mocked Skill Pack Prototype covers all marketplace functionality with local JSON state and guided work.
